title: CHB-MIT
description: The CHB-MIT Scalp EEG Database is a collection of continuous EEG recordings from 22 pediatric subjects (5 males, ages 3-22; 17 females, ages 1.5-19) with intractable epilepsy, acquired at Children's Hospital Boston. The dataset comprises 686 EEG recording files containing 198 annotated seizure events, recorded at 256 Hz using the International 10-20 electrode system with bipolar montages. Subjects were monitored for several days following anti-seizure medication withdrawal to characterize seizure patterns and assess surgical intervention candidacy. This BIDS-converted derivative preserves all original data while standardizing channel nomenclature and formatting to comply with BIDS standards.
methods_description: Continuous scalp EEG monitoring was performed on pediatric subjects (ages 1.5-22 years) with intractable seizures following anti-seizure medication withdrawal in a controlled hospital environment. Signals were sampled at 256 samples per second with 16-bit resolution using the International 10-20 system of electrode positions with bipolar montages. Most files contain 23 EEG signals; additional signals such as ECG and vagal nerve stimulus were recorded in some cases. Recording files typically contain one hour of digitized EEG, though some contain two-hour or four-hour recordings. The original EDF files from PhysioNet have been converted to BIDS format with channel names standardized to match the standard 10-05 montage naming convention.
